rule.rationale: Backend errors crossing public boundaries leak implementation contracts and unstable classification.
rule.remediation: Map the backend failure to a stable package-owned error before returning it.

backend_error_boundaries.description: Exact packages or trailing /... trees that own exported API boundaries.

backend_error_passthroughs.description: Exact wrapper results and argument positions that preserve backend errors.

backend_error_sources.description: Exact backend callables and zero-based error result positions.

overlaps.canonical_authority: errorlint owns generic wrapping and comparison mechanics; analysis owns configured backend provenance at exported boundaries
overlaps.compatible_configuration: Keep errorlint enabled for errors.Is, errors.As, and wrapping syntax.

rule.rationale: Repository-specific forbidden APIs preserve deprecated dependencies and boundary bypasses.
rule.remediation: Use the configured replacement or move the call into a reviewed adapter.

policies.description: Exact package symbols, replacements, and reviewed package exceptions.

overlaps.canonical_authority: Staticcheck owns documented deprecations; analysis owns organization migrations
overlaps.compatible_configuration: Keep SA1019 enabled and configure only organization policy here.

rule.rationale: Organization interface names communicate stable architectural roles at API boundaries.
rule.remediation: Rename the interface to match its configured role or add an exact reviewed compatibility name.

interface_names.description: Package trees with required interface prefixes, suffixes, and exact compatibility names.

overlaps.canonical_authority: ST1003 owns Go initialism spelling; analysis owns configured architectural-role affixes
overlaps.compatible_configuration: Keep ST1003 enabled and use interface naming policy only for role affixes.

rule.rationale: Provider-owned interfaces couple consumers to abstractions they do not require.
rule.remediation: Declare the smallest interface in the consuming package and accept it at the use site.

interface_provider_packages.description: Provider package trees forbidden from declaring exported value interfaces.

overlaps.canonical_authority: ireturn owns interface return policy; analysis owns configured declaration placement
overlaps.compatible_configuration: Keep ireturn enabled for return-site policy.

overlaps.canonical_authority: interfacebloat owns method-count thresholds; analysis owns configured declaration placement
overlaps.compatible_configuration: Keep interfacebloat enabled for interface breadth.

rule.rationale: Boundary bypasses couple inner packages to forbidden implementation details.
rule.remediation: Move the dependency behind an approved port or adapter.

contexts.description: Named bounded contexts and the other contexts each may import.

layers.description: Named layers and the other layers each may import.

packages.description: Non-overlapping package trees assigned to layers and contexts.

policies.description: Package trees with explicit denied and allowed imports.

restricted_dependencies.description: Backend package trees and the adapter trees allowed to import them.

overlaps.canonical_authority: analysis
overlaps.compatible_configuration: Disable overlapping depguard package rules.

rule.rationale: Blocking APIs without context cannot propagate cancellation or deadlines.
rule.remediation: Accept context.Context and propagate it through blocking operations.

policies.description: Exact packages and exported blocking API symbols.

rule.rationale: Replacing an available lifecycle with a root context loses cancellation and deadlines.
rule.remediation: Propagate the caller context or create roots only in approved composition packages.

allowed_packages.description: Exact package paths allowed to create root contexts.

rule.rationale: Struct storage obscures who owns context cancellation and lifetime.
rule.remediation: Pass context through each operation instead of retaining it.

allowed_packages.description: Exact packages with reviewed context ownership.

rule.rationale: HTTP clients without explicit positive timeouts can retain blocked operations and resources indefinitely.
rule.remediation: Set Client.Timeout to a positive reviewed duration, or isolate an intentional streaming client in an approved package.

http_timeout_exceptions.description: Exact packages allowed to construct clients without a positive timeout.

overlaps.canonical_authority: noctx owns request context; analysis owns client timeout construction
overlaps.compatible_configuration: Keep noctx enabled for outbound request context.

overlaps.canonical_authority: G114 owns HTTP server timeouts; analysis owns client timeouts
overlaps.compatible_configuration: Keep G114 enabled for HTTP servers.

rule.rationale: Process-global HTTP defaults hide connection, timeout, and shutdown ownership.
rule.remediation: Construct and inject an explicit http.Client and cloned transport with reviewed timeouts.

overlaps.canonical_authority: noctx owns contextless calls; analysis owns shared globals
overlaps.compatible_configuration: Keep noctx enabled for HTTP convenience calls.

rule.rationale: Discarding an explicit cleanup result loses resource lifetime ownership.
rule.remediation: Bind the cleanup result and call it, defer it, or transfer it to the resource owner.

resource_constructors.description: Exact callables and zero-based cleanup result positions.

overlaps.canonical_authority: bodyclose owns HTTP response bodies; analysis owns configured cleanup results
overlaps.compatible_configuration: Keep bodyclose enabled and do not configure HTTP response-body contracts here.

overlaps.canonical_authority: sqlclosecheck owns database/sql resources; analysis owns configured cleanup results
overlaps.compatible_configuration: Keep sqlclosecheck enabled and do not configure database/sql contracts here.

rule.rationale: Callbacks and blocking I/O under an internal lock can deadlock, amplify contention, and transfer lock-order control.
rule.remediation: Copy required state, release the lock, then invoke the configured callback or I/O operation.

lock_sensitive_calls.description: Exact callback or I/O callables forbidden while a lock is definitely held.

overlaps.canonical_authority: go vet owns lock copying; analysis owns configured calls under locks
overlaps.compatible_configuration: Keep copylocks enabled.

overlaps.canonical_authority: Staticcheck owns empty critical sections; analysis owns configured calls under locks
overlaps.compatible_configuration: Keep SA2001 enabled.

rule.rationale: Constructors that start goroutines hide lifetime, shutdown, and error ownership from callers.
rule.remediation: Start work explicitly after construction and expose cancellation, waiting, and error contracts.

policies.description: Exact packages and constructor function or method symbols.

rule.rationale: Goroutines started during package initialization have no caller-visible lifetime, shutdown, or error owner.
rule.remediation: Move startup behind explicit construction or Start and expose cancellation, waiting, and error contracts.

rule.rationale: Package init hides ordering, failure, and lifecycle ownership.
rule.remediation: Expose an explicit constructor called by the composition root.

allowed_packages.description: Exact package paths with reviewed init requirements.

rule.rationale: Library process termination bypasses caller-owned error handling and cleanup.
rule.remediation: Return a classified error and let an approved entrypoint terminate the process.

allowed_packages.description: Exact package import paths allowed to control the process.

rule.rationale: A transaction without immediate rollback ownership can remain open on early returns and failed commits.
rule.remediation: After the constructor error guard, immediately defer Rollback on the transaction before performing work.

transactions.description: Exact constructors, transaction result positions, and rollback methods.

overlaps.canonical_authority: sqlclosecheck owns Rows, Stmt, NamedStmt, and pgx query closure; analysis owns configured transaction rollback establishment
overlaps.compatible_configuration: Keep sqlclosecheck enabled for query and statement resources.

rule.rationale: Loop-driven goroutine launches can make concurrency scale with untrusted or unbounded work.
rule.remediation: Use a fixed worker pool, a proven bounded semaphore, or synchronous per-iteration completion.

goroutine_fanout.description: Package trees and maximum statically expanded goroutine launches.

overlaps.canonical_authority: go vet owns loop-variable capture lifetime; analysis owns configured concurrency fan-out bounds
overlaps.compatible_configuration: Keep loopclosure enabled.

overlaps.canonical_authority: Staticcheck owns WaitGroup.Add placement; analysis owns proven loop concurrency bounds
overlaps.compatible_configuration: Keep SA2000 enabled.

rule.rationale: Attacker-controlled metric label names create unbounded metric schemas and time series.
rule.remediation: Map the input to a fixed allowlist of label names or use it only as a bounded label value.

metric_label_name_sinks.description: Exact metric callables and zero-based label-name positions.

metric_label_name_types.description: Exact named types carrying attacker-controlled label names.

overlaps.canonical_authority: promlinter owns metric naming and help conventions; analysis owns typed attacker-controlled label-name flows
overlaps.compatible_configuration: Keep promlinter enabled for Prometheus conventions.

rule.rationale: High-cardinality metric labels create unbounded time series and observability cost.
rule.remediation: Replace the value with a bounded category or remove it from metric labels.

metric_label_sinks.description: Exact metric callables and zero-based label argument positions.

metric_label_types.description: Exact named types carrying high-cardinality label values.

overlaps.canonical_authority: promlinter owns metric naming and help conventions; analysis owns configured typed cardinality flows
overlaps.compatible_configuration: Keep promlinter enabled for Prometheus conventions.

rule.rationale: Composite package variables hide shared ownership and mutable process state.
rule.remediation: Construct the state explicitly and inject its owner into dependent components.

mutable_globals.description: Package trees governed by shared mutable state policy.

overlaps.canonical_authority: analysis owns typed shared mutable state; gochecknoglobals owns blanket global-style prohibition
overlaps.compatible_configuration: Disable gochecknoglobals for packages governed by this rule.

rule.rationale: Unsafe, cgo, and linkname bypass Go safety boundaries.
rule.remediation: Use a safe Go API or isolate the operation in an approved package.

overlaps.canonical_authority: analysis
overlaps.compatible_configuration: Keep G103 advisory for unsafe call detail.

rule.rationale: Sensitive typed values can leak through logs, telemetry, errors, formatting, and URLs.
rule.remediation: Pass an explicitly redacted representation or remove the value from the configured sink.

sensitive_types.description: Exact named types carrying secret-bearing values.

sinks.description: Exact callables and sensitive argument positions.

overlaps.canonical_authority: gosec owns hardcoded credentials; analysis owns typed sink flows
overlaps.compatible_configuration: Keep G101 enabled for source credential detection.
